title: Lobivia × francescae
taxonomy: Lobivia × francescae is a hybrid species of cacti, resulting from a cross between Lobivia species. This hybrid was created by the cactus breeder and collector, Francesca Baldini. As a result, its classification is primarily based on its parent species, which are part of the Lobivia genus. The hybrid's classification is further categorized within the Cactaceae family, which comprises all cacti species. Its exact classification in the botanical hierarchy is likely a combination of its parent species' classifications.

intro: Lobivia × francescae, also known as Francesca's Pincushion or Lobivia hybrid, is a hybrid cactus species belonging to the genus Lobivia, characterized by its distinctive rounded stems and vibrant flower colors. The health benefits of Lobivia × francescae include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Lobivia × francescae is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally employed to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
